executive_summary: A high-severity brute force attack targeting 'prod-server-01' was identified originating from a private IP address (192.168.7.45), linked to the nation-state threat actor APT-41. This incident poses significant risk due to the involvement of a known nation-state actor and the presence of exploitable vulnerabilities on the targeted server.
alert_details: Original alert SOC-2024-001 reported a brute force SSH attempt using username 'root' with 455 login attempts on port 4444, from IP 192.168.7.45 against 'prod-server-01'. Additional alerts within a 24-hour window show related activities: a port scan and another brute force attempt on separate targets.
triage_assessment: Given the private nature of the source IP, the alert likely indicates either a misconfiguration exposing internal resources externally or an internal threat vector. Coupled with the number of attempts and lack of intelligence on the IP, this confirms a true positive incident.
investigation_findings: Correlated with APT-41 operations, the attack aligns with known tactics involving Credential Access via brute force (MITRE Technique T1110). Furthermore, the server 'prod-server-01' hosts unpatched vulnerabilities CVE-2023-34532 and CVE-2023-68394, heightening the risk profile.
mitre_mapping: Tactic: Credential Access, Technique: Brute Force (T1110)
timeline: 10:22 UTC - Brute force attempt detected on 'prod-server-01'
